# ∈ Principle

A philosophical foundation for public responsibility in the age of generative
and autonomous systems.

## Canonical URL

https://agent-manifest.github.io/e-principle/

## Source repository

https://github.com/agent-manifest/e-principle

## Identifiers

- Repo DOI:      https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18965669
- Book (EN) DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18945860
- Book (ES) DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18945953
- Version:       1.0
- License:       CC BY 4.0
- Author:        Hernán Alfredo Capucci
- ORCID:         https://orcid.org/0009-0008-7216-3032

## Core doctrine

∈ Principle is a philosophical foundation for public responsibility in the age
of generative and autonomous systems.
It is not copyright, not a digital signature, and not a quality seal.

A work ∈ when:
1. it has an identifiable author who assumes it publicly,
2. that author is willing to answer for its content,
3. the act of assumption is explicit and verifiable.

## What ∈ is not

- Not copyright. It protects nothing. It only establishes something.
- Not a digital signature. It does not verify identity: it declares intention.
- Not a quality seal. A work can ∈ and be wrong.

## Relationship to Agent Manifest

∈ Principle is conceptually related to Agent Manifest but remains an
independent philosophical and foundational work. Agent Manifest extends
the ∈ framework into the domain of autonomous AI agents by requiring an
explicit declaration before execution.

## Citation

Capucci, H. A. (2026). ∈ Principle. Zenodo.
https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18965669
